message:
fix(build_slots): key lease file + matcher by agent identity only, not (agent, branch)

`lease_path_for_holder` and `lease_matches_holder` were using the
tuple `(agent_name, branch)` to identify a holder, but the
slot-level invariant (enforced by `existing_agent_lease_path` and
`collect_slot_conflicts`) is one-agent-one-lease per slot — branch
is descriptive metadata recorded on the lease, not part of the
identity.  When an agent acquired a slot on branch A and then
checked out branch B before renewing or releasing, the path-derived
filename and the equality check both rejected the existing lease,
so renew silently created a parallel file under a different hashed
filename and release silently no-op'd, leaking leases until the
slot was force-cleared.

Both functions now compute identity from `agent_name` alone:

- `lease_path_for_holder` hashes only the agent name, keeping the
  16-char sha256 prefix on disk stable across branch changes (the
  prefix exists to keep the on-disk name unique when
  `safe_component` strips characters from the visible agent name).

- `lease_matches_holder` compares only `lease.agent`, so a lease
  whose recorded branch has drifted from the project's current
  branch is still recognised as the agent's own.

Both `branch` parameters are now `_branch` and load-bearing only
as documentation that callers may still pass branch context for
future use; the active code path ignores it intentionally with
load-bearing comments explaining why.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
